entry.title: Re: New Learning Experience / Custom Cards: Can't edit
entry.content: Hello PL1. I can't read the KBA you shared. However, the custom cards are editable now. Thanks.

entry.title: Re: TaxFactory 11.0
entry.content:
Hello @alicia_robinson,

I have just replied you on the Year End 2024 blog.

Kind Regards,
Carlos

entry.title: The Path Forward: SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
SAP Workflow Management and SAP Intelligent RPA Are Retired: Here’s Your Next Step with SAP Build Process Automation 

As part of SAP's continuous innovation, SAP Build Process Automation has been introduced as the successor to SAP Workflow Management within the SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP). With the recent announcement of the retirement of SAP Workflow Management and SAP Intelligent RPA, now is the time for customers to plan their transition to SAP Build Process Automation. This next-generation tool is designed to bring powerful, unified capabilities for process automation, enabling you to build workflows, deploy automations, and enhance productivity across your organization.

Why Transition to SAP Build Process Automation?

With the retirement of SAP Workflow Management and SAP Intelligent RPA, SAP Build Process Automation has emerged as the comprehensive tool for creating intelligent workflows and automations. Designed to bring together workflow, RPA, and AI-driven process automation, SAP Build Process Automation enables business and IT teams to work collaboratively to simplify complex, repetitive tasks and drive efficiency like never before. 

If you're currently using SAP Workflow Management or Intelligent RPA, now is the time to explore how SAP Build Process Automation can empower your teams with its advanced capabilities. This transition also ensures ongoing support, access to the latest features, and alignment with SAP’s innovation roadmap. 

Join Our Exclusive Migration Webinars 

To help you make this transition seamless, we’re hosting a series of exclusive webinars. Our product management team will provide guidance on migrating to SAP Build Process Automation and answer any questions. You’ll gain insights into: 

  • Migration Strategies: How to move from SAP Workflow Management and Intelligent RPA to SAP Build Process Automation smoothly. 
  • Advanced Features: Explore new capabilities for workflow, RPA, and AI within the SAP Build environment. 
  • Best Practices: Get practical tips and resources to accelerate your migration journey. 

Mark your calendar and choose the session that fits your needs: 

SAP Workflow Management Migration Webinar                               SAP Intelligent RPA Migration Webinar 

📅Date: Tuesday, December 3, 2024                                                     📅  Date: Thursday, December 5, 2024 

⏰Time: 10 am EST                                                                                ⏰Time: 10 am EST   

[Register Here]                                                                                         [Register Here] 

 

Additional Resources 

To further support your migration journey, we’ve curated helpful resources: 

  • SAP Workflow Management: The Path Forward 
  • Transition to SAP Build Process Automation 
  • SAP Community Blogpost on SAP Build Process Automation 

Don’t miss this opportunity to learn how SAP Build Process Automation is transforming the automation landscape. Whether you’re in business or IT, these webinars are designed to give you the insights and support you need to make a successful transition. 

Let’s make automation simpler, smarter, and more powerful with SAP Build Process Automation. Register today to unlock the full potential of SAP Build for your organization!

entry.title: Re: Setting Up Secure SSL Connections: Pitfalls, Tips, and Tricks
entry.content:
Hello, 

if you want to deactivate SSL for short time, you should uncomment following line in the client configuration file db2cli.ini  

Security=ssl
SSLServerCertificate=<filename to arm-file>

And the DB2COMM dbm parameter should be set to TCPIP. 

Beste Regards

entry.title: SAP CPI Custom Adapter Development Spring Bean Usage
entry.content:
Hello everyone,
I am currently working on a custom adapter for the CPI. So far everything has gone smoothly. But I've been trying to register my written classes as Spring Beans in the existing Camel Context for some time now and it just doesn't want to work. Has anyone had any experience with this and can share here how it works and whether it is possible?

entry.title: Sales Order overall status
entry.content:
Hello,

I have some questions about the logic to determine the sales order status: what is the parameter for a order to be in status 'Open' or 'In Process'? I have some orders that are 'In Process' but from my point of view should be in 'Open' status.

Best regards.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Sales Order overall status
entry.content:
Hello,

For status 'Open': the sales order is still in its initial state with no further actions taken. 

For status 'In Process': the sales order has progressed beyond the initial stage, and some action or processing has already occurred.
I.e.: it has entered some stage of processing, such as credit check, delivery creation, picking, or billing, but it is not yet fully completed.

Kind regards.

entry.title: How to - Customize Receipts in SAP CCO and What to Keep in Mind
entry.content:
Basics and Common Issues in Customizing the SalesReceiptPrintTemplateA4 for SAP CCO

SAP Customer Checkout (CCO) provides standard templates for A4 and 80mm receipts. However, in some cases, customers may require specific modifications to these templates. This guide focuses on adapting the A4 format and highlights key considerations for customization.

The main modifications are applied in the SalesReceiptPrintTemplateA4.xsl file. Additional XSL files that impact this receipt format include BaseIncludes.xsl and PrintAttributesA4.xsl. Although the SalesReceiptTemplate80mm.xsl can also be modified similarly, note that adjustments may differ based on the format.

 

Structure of SalesReceiptPrintTemplateA4.xsl and Supporting Files

The SalesReceiptPrintTemplateA4.xsl for SAP CCO is built on a combination of various XSL files, each contributing specific functionality to the printing process. Two particularly important files for the A4 format are BaseIncludes.xsl and PrintAttributesA4.xsl, which determine layout, fonts, sizes, and attributes for printing on an A4 receipt.

  • BaseIncludes.xsl: This file contains essential variables and templates that form the foundation for the SalesReceiptPrintTemplateA4.xsl. For example, it defines variables for language translations, current date, and cash desk data. Additionally, the file includes formatting templates that allow flexible and region-specific content presentation.

  • PrintAttributesA4.xsl: This file sets the layout settings for the A4 receipt, such as page size (21 cm x 29.7 cm), margins, and font (Arial). Adjustments to these settings allow customization of the receipt layout to meet specific business requirements.

 

Creating root.xml to Extend Receipt Content

If customers require additional information on the receipt that is not provided by default, root.xml offers an effective way to retrieve all potential print values. This file can be generated by modifying the run.bat file within the CustomerCheckout folder. Using a text editor such as Notepad++, add the line “-DFOP= true ^” between lines 226 and 268.

It is important to note that root.xml will only be generated if the A4 receipt is configured to print as a PDF. Once created, the file allows for identifying the path to the desired values, which can then be integrated into the SalesReceiptPrintTemplateA4.xsl to customize the receipt.

 

Step 1:

Step 2:

Step 3:

 

Tips for Implementing Changes

  • Restart the POS After Changes: After making changes to the SalesReceiptPrintTemplateA4.xsl, you need to restart the POS and print a receipt (a PDF print is sufficient) to ensure that the adjustments have been correctly applied. Although time-consuming, this is the only way to verify functionality.

  • Troubleshooting: If the template prints in the default format or fails to print, there is likely an error in the modifications. The console, which opens when the POS starts, may display error messages that help in troubleshooting. It is recommended to save successful adjustments to avoid reverting to faulty changes.

  • Important Note on Integrating with the POS: Once modifications to SalesReceiptTemplateA4.xsl are successful, the file should be saved in a folder. It is essential to rename the file here to prevent it from being overwritten during system updates, which could cause all changes to be lost. You must also update the POS configuration to reference the new file name. This applies to all modified files in the folder, including BaseIncludes.xsl and other print templates.

 

Conclusion

Customizing the SalesReceiptPrintTemplateA4.xsl for SAP CCO can be a complex task, but it offers ample opportunities to tailor receipts to meet customer requirements. With a solid understanding of the XSL files involved, proper configuration of root.xml, and adherence to best practices for modifications and troubleshooting, you can create customized receipts that are both functional and visually appealing.

entry.title: How to change the node ID of Cronjobs
entry.content:
When cluster ID auto discovery is enabled with cluster.nodes.autodiscovery=true, each node has a randomly assigned ID after every startup. Usually, frontend nodes will have the task engine disabled with task.engine.loadonstartup=false.
If a nodeId is explicitly set for a CronJob or Job, there may be cases where the assigned nodeId ends up linked to a node that cannot run tasks or CronJobs. For example, in the screenshot below, we have a Job with nodeId=13:

In this example, nodeId=13 corresponds to a frontend node where the task engine is disabled. As a result, the CronJob will not run on this node.
This will most often happen in systems that were migrated to SAP Commerce Cloud, where auto discovery is automatically enabled. In previous systems, you may have explicitly set the nodeId to a value that was always assigned to a dedicated backend server having for example cluster.id=13.

Resolution:

  • When using cluster ID auto-discovery, the nodeId associated with tasks should always be set to null. This ensures that CronJobs are scheduled dynamically based on the available nodes.
    The solution is to delete the problematic CronJob and recreate it. By default, when a new CronJob is created in SAP Commerce Cloud, the NodeID is set to null.
  • However, if there are too many CronJobs or Jobs with the nodeId explicitly set, and you want to avoid recreating each one manually, you can use the following SQL query to bulk update the nodeId for all Jobs where the nodeId is not null:

    UPDATE jobs
    SET p_nodeid = null
    WHERE p_nodeid is not null

Make sure that Commit mode is enabled, otherwise, the above query will be rolled back and have no effect.

  • Important: After you ran the SQL Query successfully, the cache should be cleaned for each one of the pods. Please do the following:
  1. Go to the HAC/Administration Console
  2. Monitoring
  3. Cache
  4. Click on Clean Cache

entry.title: Re: How to manage sequence of functions in SAP PaPM Cloud?
entry.content:
Hello Sergio,

I believe you can achieve this using Rule Hierarchy:

"Join is a data access function that brings together the results of two or more other functions based on defined rules. Each join rule semantically defines the reading of a specific input. Hierarchical join rules are also supported by assigning higher levels. The hierarchy of levels is resolved starting with the highest level, feeding as input to the lower levels and ending with level 0."

But depending on the type of writer you need to sequence, a different solution was necessary in my tests.

Using "Insert" writer type:

Consider this model as example:

Starting with three identical model tables, where initially only 'Model Table 1' (MT01) contains data.

- Writer 1 (W01) copies data from MT01 to MT02.
- Writer 2 (W02) copies data from MT02 to MT03.

Writer 2 should only run after Writer 1 is complete. Writers functions are flagged as 'Sub Function' in  Parallel Processing Type setup.

The 'Join - Execution' (J01) will be the executable function. The hierarchy in Join Rules ensures Writer 1 runs before Writer 2, so new entries in MT01 are first written to MT02 and then to MT03.

Additionally, an invalid filter can be applied in the Join Output subview to avoid unnecessary data replication in the J01 underlying Y Hana table (so the function output will be empty).

In my tests this setup respected the desired order of execution. Here is the message log (for a second run) of the Join Function.

 

Using "Delete and Insert" writer type:

This one is a little trickier, because when a "delete and insert" writer is defined as a sub-function/input for Join, it only performs the insert part. More details on this can be found here: PaPM Writer: Delete and Insert - SAP Community.

When you change the writer to "Executable," it will perform the delete part properly, but run in parallel (the sequence in Join Rules is not respected).

To make this work, I created a new Model Table 4 (MT04) with the same structure, which will be left empty and used to clear MT02 and MT03.

Now we have MT04 as input for the new Writer 0A (Output is MT02) and Writer 0B (Output is MT03), which will run first in our Join Rules Hierarchy, ensuring the previous results are cleared. These writers will be flagged as Executable, since there is no problem if they run in parallel.

Writers 1 and 2 should now be flagged as sub-functions, since they will be inserting data into empty model tables and we no longer need the delete part to function. This will ensure that the Join Rules Hierarchy is respected for the writing part.

 

With this setup, I was able to achieve the desired output. Find below the run messages and timestamps:

Regards,
Leonardo Silva

entry.title: Variable Order Units in Procurement Process
entry.content:
This blog outlines the steps to configure Var.OUn's and the process flow involved in utilizing them effectively.

Step 1: Create Material (Tcode: MM01)

In Purchasing view select variable order unit as “2 – Active with own price”

Maintain Alternative units and conversion factor under units of measure tab.

(Navigation path: Additional Data --> Units of Measure)

Maintain all the relevant material data and save the material, 12345 material saved.

Step 2: Create Purchase Info Record (Tcode: ME11)

Maintain Net price per 1 unit (50 USD for 1 PC)

(This price will be reflected in the PB00 condition type under the conditions tab in the purchase order)

Click conditions, maintain price for all unit of measure by choosing one by one.

Order unit BAG (5,000 USD for 1 BAG)

(This price will be reflected in the PB00 condition type under the conditions tab in the purchase order)

Order unit CARTON (50,000 USD for 1 CAR)

(This price will be reflected in the PB00 condition type under the conditions tab in the purchase order)

Once relevant data maintained save the info record.

PIR 5300005187 Created.

Step 3: Pre-requisite check to reflect the prices under PB00 condition type

Check 1: Access sequence under the PB00 Condition type (Tcode: M/06)

Check 2: Table 066 & 067 must exist under the access sequence (Tcode: M/07)

(If custom access sequence created make sure these two tables must exist in that)

Step 4: Create Purchase Order (Tcode: ME21N)

Order unit as PC, 50 USD reflected under PB00 condition type.

Order unit as BAG, 5,000 USD reflected under PB00 condition type

Order unit as CAR, 50,000 USD reflected under PB00 condition type

PO 19001096 saved for 1 CARTON.

Step 5: Goods Receipt and Invoice posted

(You can check the GR & IR document in purchase order history tab)

entry.title: Re: New Learning Experience / Custom Cards: Can't edit
entry.content: Interesting. The KBA is externally available. It might be necessary to review your S-User access - it's possible to open a case under the "XX-SER-FORME" component for it.

I'm sure if needed we get the licensing sorted out eventually but when we need to implement business functions, bgRFC and whatnot (I saw when skimming the docs) on a short notice, I probably need to clone a basis guy 😎

Cheers

PS: Finally a reply to a question in the "new" community 🎉 even if it was a tounge-in-cheek one.

entry.title: Re: New Learning Experience / Custom Cards: Can't edit
entry.content: It doesn't say that it is off-limits, it just says it's "Unable to fetch data at the moment. This might be due to a server error or a network issue. Please try again later."

entry.title: Re: Compensation: Can Salary Range be displayed in annual...
entry.content:
Hi @sandyhonigsberg 

We need more information to be able to provide guidance. 

  1. Is your template getting the salary range information from EC ? you can check in Plan Setup > Settings

     

  2. Is your template configured with Periodic Planning or Annualized Planning (#1 or #2 from this the examples in the first paragraphs of this blog). The display of salary ranges goes hand in hand with these design options.


All the best

Xavier

entry.title: Re: Statutory Report in S/4HANA 2023 On Prem without (full fledged) DRC
entry.content: Regarding the question of content on the "full" licensing I would imagine legal changes would be delivered how you suggest, but I would be merely speculating. Far too much stuff changes names/acronyms in the SAP landscape along with feature changes to keep up with what the path forward would look like.

entry.title: Re: S4 Upgrade: TCI SAPK10601ACPS4CORE not available

entry.title: How to run the app Source Inspections - Worklist ( QI07 ) in Background in S/4HANA CLoud ?
entry.content:
In the App: "Source Inspections - Worklist", menu--> Program the option "Execute in Background" is disabled.

We want this to be triggered in background, we need this option to put background job schedule, how can we do this in S/4HANA Cloud ?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to run the app Source Inspections - Worklist ( QI...
entry.content:
The app Source Inspections - Worklist  ( QI07  ) is only available currently for running in foreground 

 

If you would like it enhanced to allow to be scheduled in background jobs then please follow the KBA 2963059 ( https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/2963059 ) and submit a feature request

entry.title: DQM Address Validation - How to stop address validation from via interface
entry.content:
Hello,

I would like the address validation to stop triggering via interface (ie. a transaction like Business Partner create via BTP) - we would only like it to trigger when an actual user is typing in the address manually via GUI.  Is there a config setting we can make to handle this?

 

Thanks, Mike

entry.title: Employment Info/ Event Reason Audit Report
entry.content:
Hi there, 

I have converted most Canvas and Table reports into Story reports, in anticipation for the complete switch over to Story reports. 

It is disappointing to find that Story Reports do not allow me to make employment info audit reports. We regularly monitor event reason specifically to ensue the correct even reasons are being chosen. Thankfully, the Table report is reliable and gives me the audit information. 

When can we expect to see Story reports to be able to run audit reports ? 

Thanks 
Aisling

entry.title: Re: How to create a segmentation profile where contacts h...
entry.content:
@ShoaibPatelbooru wrote:

I need to create a Segmentation Profile and filter only those contacts which either contains country information or Marketing Area information. How can I do it?

 

In SAP Marketing Cloud, create a segmentation profile by adding Country and Marketing Area attributes. Set the filter condition to "OR" between them, so contacts with either attribute will be included. Save, activate, and test to confirm correct filtering.

entry.title: Re: SAP FICO Certification
entry.content:
Hi @nsairam
Have you checked the How to prepare section.
The content you will find there provides you with in-depth knowledge of the topic, and provides you with the option to become certified.
Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Health Monitoring with Cloud ALM - Custom Metrics for S/4 HANA private cloud Part 2 (ABAP)
entry.content:
Introduction

In my previous blog, I described how to enable the Cloud ALM Inbound Metrics API service and test it with Postman. In this blog I will explain the details on how to achieve the same result from an ABAP test class.

Call the Cloud ALM API from ABAP

Disclaimer: the code in this blog doesn't include any error handling and has all the parameters hardcoded. I might upload runnable version to GitHub if I have something a cleaner and generic. I wanted to build a mini RAP framework around it and make it open source, but I'll leave it to someone else with more time.

Get the API token

  • The first step is to save the API service key authentication details as an SM59 HTTP (Type G) destination. Use the service key client ID and secret in the logon tab "Basic Authentication" fields.

  • Fetch the token string using the SM59 destination and parse it using the xco_cp_json library or any other option available in your system. I used this one because is the only released option in my S/4 2023 system.

 

method get_token.
    data: begin of ls_token,
            access_token type string,
            token_type type string,
            expires_in type i,
            scope type string,
            jti type string,
          end of ls_token.

    data(lv_calm_dest) = 'CLOUD_ALM_INBOUND_METRICS_API'.
    cl_http_client=>create_by_destination(
      exporting
        destination              = lv_calm_dest
      importing
        client                   = data(lr_http_client)
      exceptions
        others                   = 1 ).

    lr_http_client->request->set_method( method = 'POST' ).
    cl_http_utility=>set_query( request = lr_http_client->request query = 'grant_type=client_credentials' ).

    lr_http_client->send( exceptions others = 1 ).
    lr_http_client->receive( exceptions others = 1 ).
    lr_http_client->response->get_status(
      importing
        code   = data(lv_code)
        reason = data(lv_reason) ).
    data(lv_bin) = lr_http_client->response->get_data( ).
    data(lr_conv) = cl_abap_conv_in_ce=>create( input = lv_bin ).
    data lv_response type string.
    lr_conv->read( importing data = lv_response ).
    lr_http_client->close( ).
    xco_cp_json=>data->from_string( iv_string = lv_response )->write_to( REF #( ls_token ) ).
    rv_token = ls_token-access_token.
    ##TODO "It could be possible to save the token expiration to keep it in cache until it's valid
  endmethod.

 

Get the Unix timestamp nano in ABAP

The Unix timestamp is required for the gauge metric to specify the point in time.

 

  method get_timestamp_nano.
    data(lv_ts_seconds) = xco_cp=>sy->unix_timestamp( )->value.
    rv_ts_nano = lv_ts_seconds * 1000000000.
  endmethod.

 

Build parameters and headers key/value tables

Use the tihttpnvp table type of the http client to populate the parameters and headers.

 

  method get_raw_metrics_api_params.
    rt_params = value #( ( name = 'useCase'   value = 'hm' )
                         ( name = 'version'   value = 'v1' )
                         ( name = 'serviceId' value = '<YOUR system ID>' )
                         ( name = 'dev'    value = 'true' ) "set to false for prod!
                         ( name = 'format'    value = 'protobuf-json' )
                          ).
  endmethod.

  method get_calm_api_headers.
    rt_headers = value #( ( name = '~request_method'   value = if_http_request=>co_request_method_post )
                          ( name = 'Content-Type'   value = 'application/json' )
                          ( name = 'Accept' value = '*/*' )
                          ( name = 'Authorization'    value = |Bearer { get_token( ) }| ) ).
  endmethod.

 

Build the body JSON object ABAP structure

Here I used the XCO_CP_JSON released library. Since the body schema is quite complex, I asked our friend ChatGPT to define the necessary ABAP structures for me from the Postman example. And guess what, it was done correctly at the first attempt! I only had to ask it to replace CamelCase by Underscore in the fields names as a 2nd step, of course you can keep the JSON notation if you prefer.

 

TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_value,
         string_value TYPE string,
       END OF ty_value.

TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_attributes,
         key   TYPE string,
         value TYPE ty_value,
       END OF ty_attributes.

TYPES: ty_attributes_tab TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_attributes WITH EMPTY KEY.

TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_data_points,
         time_unix_nano TYPE string,
         as_double     TYPE f,
         attributes   TYPE ty_attributes_tab,
       END OF ty_data_points.

TYPES: ty_data_points_tab TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_data_points WITH EMPTY KEY.

TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_gauge,
         data_points TYPE ty_data_points_tab,
       END OF ty_gauge.

TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_metrics,
         name        TYPE string,
         description TYPE string,
         gauge       TYPE ty_gauge,
       END OF ty_metrics.

TYPES: ty_metrics_tab TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_metrics WITH EMPTY KEY.

TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_scope,
         name    TYPE string,
         version TYPE string,
       END OF ty_scope.

TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_scope_metrics,
         scope   TYPE ty_scope,
         metrics TYPE ty_metrics_tab,
       END OF ty_scope_metrics.

TYPES: ty_scope_metrics_tab TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_scope_metrics WITH EMPTY KEY.

TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_resource,
         attributes TYPE ty_attributes_tab,
       END OF ty_resource.

TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_resource_metrics,
         resource      TYPE ty_resource,
         scope_metrics  TYPE ty_scope_metrics_tab,
       END OF ty_resource_metrics.

TYPES: ty_resource_metrics_tab TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_resource_metrics WITH EMPTY KEY.

TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_calm_in_metrics_body,
         resource_metrics TYPE ty_resource_metrics_tab,
       END OF ty_calm_in_metrics_body.

 

Fill the metrics body structure

You just need to declare the body structure and fill the necessary fields. Normally you would have multiple "metrics" records and multiple "data points" for the metrics that require it. But in this example I only write one metric (SM13 errors) and 1 data point (client 300):

 

data lv_body_abap TYPE ty_calm_in_metrics_body.
data lv_sm13_errors_300 type int8 value '8.0'.

data(lv_resource_metrics) = VALUE ty_resource_metrics(
  resource = VALUE ty_resource(
    attributes = VALUE ty_attributes_tab( (
      key = 'service.name'
      value = VALUE ty_value( string_value = 'S4D300' )
    ) )
  )
  scope_metrics = VALUE ty_scope_metrics_tab( ( 
    scope = VALUE ty_scope(
      name = 'HM_ANALYTICS_DP'
      version = 'V1'
    )
    metrics = VALUE ty_metrics_tab( (
      name        = 'Custom.SM13.Errors'
      description = 'SM13 Update Requests with Errors'
      gauge = VALUE ty_gauge(
        data_points = VALUE ty_data_points_tab( (
          time_unix_nano = lv_ts_nano_str
          as_double     = lv_sm13_errors_300
          attributes   = VALUE ty_attributes_tab( (
            key   = 'Client'
            value = VALUE ty_value( string_value = '300' )
          ) ( 
            key   = 'Instance'
            value = VALUE ty_value( string_value = 's4d-pas-00' )
          ) (
            key   = 'Sid'
            value = VALUE ty_value( string_value = 'S4D' )
          ) )
        ) )
      )
    ) )
  ) )
).

lv_body_abap-resource_metrics = VALUE ty_resource_metrics_tab( ( lv_resource_metrics ) ).

 

Build the JSON body

Now you only need to transform the ABAP structure into a JSON object. Since I used underscores for the ABAP field names, now I have to transform to camelCase. I recommend comparing the JSON string from Postman with the one generated with the ABAP code to validate the result.

 

data(lv_body_json) = xco_cp_json=>data->from_abap( ia_abap = lv_resource_metrics )->apply( 
  VALUE #( ( xco_cp_json=>transformation->underscore_to_camel_case ) ) )->to_string( ).

 

Call the API

Finally you only need to call the API with the HTTP client and read the response. If you set the "dev" parameter to "true" you can get more details in case of issues.

 

  method if_oo_adt_classrun~main.
    cl_http_client=>create_by_url(
      exporting
        url                = |https://eu10.alm.cloud.sap/api/calm-metrics/v1/metrics|
      importing
        client             = data(lo_http_client)
      exceptions
        others             = 1 ).
    if sy-subrc <> 0.
      "error handling
    endif.

    "setting request method, parameters and headers
    lo_http_client->request->set_method( if_http_request=>co_request_method_post ).
    lo_http_client->request->set_form_fields( fields = get_raw_metrics_api_params( ) ).
    lo_http_client->request->set_header_fields( fields = get_calm_api_headers( ) ).

    lo_http_client->request->set_cdata( exporting data = lv_body ).
    lo_http_client->send( exceptions others = 1 ).
    if sy-subrc <> 0.
      "error handling
    else.
      lo_http_client->receive(
        exceptions
          others                     = 1 ).
    endif.
    data(lv_response) = lo_http_client->response->get_cdata( ).
    lo_http_client->close( ).
    out->write( |response: { lv_response }| ).
  endmethod.

 

Run the test class and validate the result in Cloud ALM

Just build a class implementing the if_oo_dat_classrun interface and test the code.

entry.title: Re: MDK Custom client build failed
entry.content: I would suggest upgrading to a newer MDK version plugin combination and trying with that

entry.title: Re: SAP Commissions - Under the hood of Stage-hooks
entry.content: • Is this Stagehook process available in SAP Incentive Management on the GCP platform? If yes, what kind of processes does it support (Java class, bash script file, stored procedure etc.)

entry.title: Re: How to create a segmentation profile where contacts h...
entry.content: Hi, No it doesn't work like it. If you use 2 attributes then it as per standard design gets treated as AND condition.

entry.title: Re: Table for stocks from storage types with level of ava...
entry.content:
Hi 

As per my knowledge,As we select available stock at bin level in a storage type. After that we dont find bin in /scwm/AQUA table.

As I dont know a direct table to link it but by logic we can find data

We have /SCWM/QUAN (Quant Attributes) this table is linked to available stock and Handling unit tables. It is linked via GUID_HU, GUID_Parent and GUID_Stock.

Handling Units (HUs) tracked by /SCWM/HUHDR are also connected through the Quant Attributes table through GUID_HU

Use  /SCWM/QUAN and /SCWM/HUHDR and you will find hu details. 

now indirectly, you can fetch hu details. 

Hope it may give some idea.

 

Regards

Rashid Khan

entry.title: Re: How to visualize images in SAP Analytics Cloud tables based on SAP Datasphere data

Best regards,
Adem

entry.title: Re: Scripting Tracker - Decoupling the Recorder to use it in Other Contexts

as far as I understand you right, you want to capture only the events from the SAP GUI. This is not an option of the recorder. But you can find an approach to realize your own recorder here: SAP GUI Scripting Recorder with Powershell.

Best regards
Stefan

entry.title: Mass action in compensation is not working as expected
entry.content:
Dear SF Compensation Experts, @xavierlegarrec ,

 

When we try to apply Mass action in compensation, and we enter the Increase/Decrease as any value, the sum of the increase/decrease is not coming equal to the number that we want to apply.

does it work with filters and the total value that we enter is applied only to Filters users or to all users in the compensation template.

Does it includes changes applied earlier as well?

 

To describe better: we entered a Mass action to Increase 100 Euros for only 2 filtered only.

Emp A: Current Merit is 27, new merit is coming as 31 only

Emp B: Current Merit is 41, new merit is coming as 45 Only

 

So the increase is in a way only 8 Euros but our ask was to increase 100 Euros. Why it is not working as expected.

 

Many thanks in advance

entry.title: Populate "Created by" and "Created on" in the Input ready query
entry.content:
There is a query where user will update the Master data for the attributes of Material , and it should populate the fields Created by and Created on in the Report whenever a new entry is made by any user 

How we can achieve this ?

entry.title: Re: 4.3 Web Intelligence Data Lineage Challenge
entry.content:
Hi

We plan to add a tooltip with the object lineage so you easily identify where the object is coming from.

About the object being renamed in both queries, this is the expected behavior and it is not related to Data mode but object rename introduced in SAP BI 4.3 SP2.

Indeed, to optimize and avoid uploading the same universe several times, the universe data source is "factorized". So if you modify one universe object, it impacts all queries that use it. You can see it in the graph in Data mode since all queries are linked to the same universe "node". This is different for Excel and Text data sources for which this factorization is not done. 

Regards, 

Christian - SAP

entry.title: Re: Become an Expert Peer for SAP Support Programs!
entry.content:

I hope you're doing well.

I have a few questions regarding the SAP expertise needed on your platform:

SAP ERP Finance

SAP Upgrade

SAP SuccessFactors Learning Modules

SAP Ariba Sourcing Events

SAP Business Procurement Administration

SAP Hana


Could you please let me know how many experts are required for the SAP products listed above? The reason I’m asking is that some of my friends have experience with these SAP products. Additionally, it's been three weeks since your post was made. Has SAP’s platform already hired chat experts, or are there still vacancies available for experts in the mentioned SAP products?

entry.title: Excel file generated via xco_cp_xlsx is not opening when sent over Email
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I am trying to generate a excel with the help of xco_cp_xlsx and attached to email. But when trying the access the attachment in Email getting the below error and unable to open the excel.

 

Code Snippet:

TRY.

DATA(lo_write_access) = xco_cp_xlsx=>document->empty( )->write_access( ).

DATA(lo_worksheet) = lo_write_access->get_workbook( )->worksheet->at_position( 1 ).

DATA(lo_cursor) = lo_worksheet->cursor(
io_column = xco_cp_xlsx=>coordinate->for_alphabetic_value( 'A' )
io_row = xco_cp_xlsx=>coordinate->for_numeric_value( 1 )
).

lo_cursor->get_cell( )->value->write_from( 'PO Number' ).
lo_cursor->move_right( )->get_cell( )->value->write_from( 'PO Item' ).
lo_cursor->move_right( )->get_cell( )->value->write_from( 'Material' ).
lo_cursor->move_right( )->get_cell( )->value->write_from( 'Net Price' ).
lo_cursor->move_right( )->get_cell( )->value->write_from( 'Qty Requested' ).
lo_cursor->move_right( )->get_cell( )->value->write_from( 'Percent Complete' ).
lo_cursor->move_right( )->get_cell( )->value->write_from( 'Gross Amount' ).
lo_cursor->move_right( )->get_cell( )->value->write_from( 'Amount to Accrue' ).


DATA(lo_selection_pattern) = xco_cp_xlsx_selection=>pattern_builder->simple_from_to(
)->from_column( xco_cp_xlsx=>coordinate->for_alphabetic_value( 'A' )
)->from_row( xco_cp_xlsx=>coordinate->for_numeric_value( 2 )
)->get_pattern( ).

lo_worksheet->select( lo_selection_pattern
)->row_stream(
)->operation->write_from( REF #( lt_purchase_orders )
)->set_value_transformation( xco_cp_xlsx_write_access=>value_transformation->best_effort
)->execute( ).

DATA(lv_file_content) = lo_write_access->get_file_content( ).

lv_email_subject = 'Accrual Report for PO Items to be Posted for Current Month'.

DATA(lv_sender_email) = 'dummy@email.com'.
lv_recipient = 'dummy@email.com'.

DATA(lo_mail) = cl_bcs_mail_message=>create_instance( ).


lo_mail->set_sender( CONV #( lv_sender_email ) ).
lo_mail->add_recipient( CONV #( lv_recipient ) ).
lo_mail->set_subject( CONV #( lv_email_subject ) ).

DATA(lv_content) = |<h1 style="font-family:Helvetica;color:#000000;">Posting Data for the current Month</h1>| &
|<p style="font-family:Helvetica;font-size:14px;font-style:normal;font-weight:normal;color:#000000;">Dear Team,<br/><br/>| &
|Please check the attached file for the Purchase Order to be posting in the current month.

lo_mail->set_main( cl_bcs_mail_textpart=>create_instance(
iv_content = lv_content
iv_content_type = 'text/html'
) ).


lo_mail->add_attachment( cl_bcs_mail_binarypart=>create_instance(
iv_content = lv_file_content
iv_content_type = 'application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet'
iv_filename = 'Posting Document.xlsx'
) ).
lo_mail->send( ).

This I am trying to achieve in S/4 HANA 2023. Please let me know if you have any pointer to resolve this issue.

Regards,

Guru

This way we can check a few things like the EUR being the functional currency for the template and more importantly whether you have hard guidelines or budget stops that could explain the behavior.

 

 

All the best

Xavier

entry.content:

Is there any way to create a condition with multiple values? Like purchasing group in 'PGR1, PGR2, PGR3'?

I understand you can use alternate (OR) conditions but it means repeating the AND in each OR... So I would like to have a condition where I can put a list of purchasing groups for example.

Any idea?

entry.title: Intercompany credit memo
entry.content:
Hi ,

Intercompany credit memo is not working even CR to IG copy control is maintained with routing 013.

When trying to create the intercompany billing IG system is showing "full quantity invoice".  When I am selecting IG manually while creating the Billing document then system is allowing to create IG document but in this case sales area and payer got copied from credit memo request but here payer and should should be different.

All the required settings is maintained in system.

Please suggest your valuable inputs.

entry.title: Re: How to manage sequence of functions in SAP PaPM Cloud?
entry.content:
Hi Leonardo,

Thank you for your effort and for sharing these solutions.

I've analyzed my model to see if I could implement your suggestions, but unfortunately, my setup introduces some additional challenges.

In my case, each of the three Writer functions has multiple dependent functions behind it—such as additional Joins, Writers, Iterative Views, and Joins that read data from Model Tables. I emphasize "Iterative Views" because they require executable functions as input, and these inputs are often Writers. This creates a need to execute the three primary Writer functions in a specific order, while also ensuring that each Writer’s dependencies are processed sequentially, whether they’re set as Sub-Functions or Executable.

In the On-Premise version of PaPM, I managed to resolve this by assigning ascending IDs to the Executable (Delete and Insert) Writer functions. For ordering independent steps executed by each Writer—especially when Iterative Views were involved—I included Iterative Views with a loop from 0 to 0, assigning ascending IDs to these views. This method proved reliable in the long term, but unfortunately, it doesn't work the same way in PaPM Cloud.

Thank you again for your help, and I hope you might have further suggestions to navigate this complexity.

Have a great weekend!

Best regards,
Sergio

entry.title: Re: Electronic Invoices for Malaysia: A Guide for Communication Settings
entry.content:

entry.title: Re: When on Nov. 15, 2024 will the 2H 2024 Production changes will be completed?
entry.content: Hello, please refer to this blog for release upgrade windows: https://community.sap.com/t5/product-and-customer-updates/2h-2024-release-upgrade-windows/ba-p/13731367

entry.title: SAP Activate road map viewer Now Accessible via SAP for Me
entry.content:
Dear all,

We are excited to announce that the SAP Activate Roadmap Viewer has been rebranded as the SAP Activate road map viewer and is now available directly through SAP for Me.

Fig 1: SAP Activate road map viewer

 

Please check out this blog for more details on the new features of the SAP Activate road map viewer.

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

1. How do I find the SAP Activate road map viewer tile on SAP for Me?

To locate the SAP Activate Road Map Viewer tile, navigate to "Services & Support" within SAP for Me and select "ALM."

Please remember that you need to authenticate using your registered SAP user account.

Fig 2: SAP for Me

 

You can also access the SAP Activate road map viewer via this Link.

2. How can I view SAP Activate road map viewer content in different languages?

To change the language of the content, click on your profile picture, go to "My Settings," and select your preferred language under "Portal Preferences."

Fig 3: User Settings

Then select your preferred language under "Portal Preferences."

Fig 4: Language Settings

 

3. What should I do if I see an almost empty screen when accessing the SAP Activate road map viewer?

If you encounter a nearly blank screen without any specific error message, this could be due to third-party cookies being blocked in your web browser.

Fig 5: Error Message

 

Please turn off your browser's third-party cookie-blocking setting and try again. If you are experiencing issues with Safari, we recommend trying Google Chrome or Microsoft Edge.

 

Your voice matters!
If you want to learn more and actively engage with SAP subject matter experts on SAP Activate Cloud Deployment Methodology, join our SAP Activate Community. This platform, which is available to all customers and partners, has a clear mission: to deliver an interactive community to engage with one another about implementation deliverables and tasks, best practices, and product solutions.

SAP Activate additional links:
• SAP S/4HANA Cloud Community
• SAP Activate Community
• SAP Activate Minute Playlist
• SAP Activate on SAP.com

I checked multiple times , got checked with my team as well, the sequence of fields is same. No common associations between 2 Union all selects . Still the error doesn’t go away.

please find below screenshot and help me out. I don’t find much blogs on this topic.

entry.title: Re: Payroll Canada Year End 2024
entry.content:
@VEH2 the standard solution involves updating the Infotypes.
There is the utility tool which I mentioned in last reply that you can use to mass update the infotypes.

Or you can use different BPA determination method . For more details on that, see Note 2982019

entry.title: Re: Payroll Canada Year End 2024
entry.content:
@rory27 Rory, if I am not mistaken the 'Box B.B' in Relevé (for additional QPP contributions) will receive the amount from /317.
Necessary changes and forms will be deliver through Year End and Notes will be listed in the Main Note.

entry.title: Using secondary data source in Fiori building blocks
entry.content:
Hi everyone!

I'm developing an app that has multiple data sources, the app is a List report floorplan that opens a dialog fragment with a macros:FilterBar component inside it.

The List report uses the default model pointing to /primaryService/MainEntity and the filterBar needs to consume /secondaryService/OtherEntity, however the filterBar component fails because is trying to find /OtherEntity in /primaryService path.

Is there a way to bind the dialog or the inner filterBar to use the secondaryModel?


Some code for context.

Dialog.fragment.xml

 

<core:FragmentDefinition  id="dialogFragment" xmlns="sap.m" xmlns:core="sap.ui.core" xmlns:mvc="sap.ui.core.mvc" xmlns:macros="sap.fe.macros" >
    <Dialog title="Custom Form" id="formDialog">
        <!-- Other unrelated fields -->
        <macros:FilterBar filterChanged="handler.onFilterChange" id="FilterBar" metaPath="/OtherEntity/@com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1.SelectionFields" />
    </Dialog>
</core:FragmentDefinition>

 

button.js - function that opens the modal

 

async function openModal(oEvent) {
    const oView = this.editFlow.getView();
    const oController = oView.getController();
    const extension = oController.getExtensionAPI()
    var oModel = this.getModel("secondaryModel");
    oView.setModel(oModel, "secondaryModel");
    const initialBindingContext = new sap.ui.model.Context(oModel, "/OtherEntity")
    oView.setBindingContext(initialBindingContext, "secondaryModel")
    let dialog = await extension.loadFragment({
        contextPath: "/OtherEntity",
        id: 'fragmentDialogId',
        name: "jobui.ext.fragment.FormDialog",
        controller: this,
        initialBindingContext: initialBindingContext
    })
    oView.addDependent(dialog)
    dialog.open()
}

 

 manifest.json (simplified)

 

{
    "sap.app": {
      "dataSources": {
        "mainService": {
          "uri": "odata/mainService/",
          "type": "OData",
          "settings": {
            "annotations": [],
            "odataVersion": "4.0"
          }
        },
        "secondaryService": {
          "uri": "/odata/secondaryService/",
          "type": "OData",
          "settings": {
            "odataVersion": "4.0"
          }
        }
      }
    },
    "sap.ui5": {
        "": {
          "dataSource": "mainService",
          "preload": true,
          "settings": {
          }
        },
        "secondaryModel": {
          "dataSource": "secondaryService",
          "preload": true,
          "settings": {
        }
    } 
  }

entry.title: Issue with Guest User Provisioning to SAP IAS
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

We are experiencing an issue where guest users are not being provisioned to SAP Identity Authentication Service (IAS). The interface between Microsoft Azure and SAP IAS provisions all regular employees at 15-minute intervals; however, it does not provision external users, who are registered as guest users in Azure.

This limitation prevents us from managing guest user permissions within SAP IAS. While guest users can log in to applications such as the Build Workzone, we are required to manually assign their permissions directly on the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP).

Additionally, these guest users appear on the BTP in a cryptic format as so-called shadow users. Instead of the standard user IDs, they are represented with an encrypted form. A screenshot from SAP BTP illustrating this issue is attached below.

Screenshot from SAP BTP

 

We kindly request your assistance in resolving this issue to ensure that guest users are properly provisioned to SAP IAS and their permissions can be centrally managed.

Thank you in advance for your support.

entry.title: Re: Providing Value Helps in Flexible Workflow Condition Parameters
entry.content:
Hello,

Has anyone tried to create a condition, like purchasing group, where you could select multiple purchasing groups and check that your PO purchasing group is in that list? Is it even possible to to have that?

All blog I see only work with a single value, so if you have multiple it would mean use alternative (OR) set of conditions, and you would have to repeat the AND conditions on each of them. Definitely not the best approach..

So any ideas anyone?

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: XSLT Transformation and standard text for ROUTING_CREATE
entry.content:
The XSLT transformation is moving the xml tags into the Idoc segments, through CALL TRANSFORMATION.
After that, I've a  CALL FUNCTION 'EDI_DATA_INCOMING' for the Idoc creation.
In the transformation, among the others, I'll need to properly fill-in the segments for texts in ROUTING_CREATE

entry.title: Re: More Flexible Conditions to Trigger a Workflow
entry.content:
Hello,

Has anyone been able to create a condition with multiple values (a list of values)?

All the blogs I've seen so far are only creating conditions with a single value.

I'm trying to find a way to create conditions with a list of values to check against.

Example: company has 10 purchasing groups, half of which should trigger one workflow and the rest trigger another workflow. How can I set a condition where I can list those 5 purchasing groups instead of creating alternate (OR) conditions (where I would have to repeat all the other AND conditions for each OR).

Is there a way to do this? Any ideas?

entry.title: Re: CDS View for Planned cost and revenue.
entry.content:
Hello. With Custom CDS view, you can merge data from various sources to model the data in accordance with your specific business requirements.
C_EngmntProjCostQuery Engagement Project Cost and Revenue Query : CDS view provides essential data such as the total planned and actual costs and revenues of a project, both overall and up to the current month. Additionally, it enables inquiries into the billed revenue of the project over the same periods and assists in estimating the probable margin percentage upon project completion. Please note that questions related to revenue and margin are not applicable to internal projects.

In addition you can learn more with help documentation on CDS Views here on CDS Views for Customer and Internal Project Management  and for enterprise project refer to Help documentation

Additionally you can refer to below CDS Views/queries for designing your solution

C_EngmntProjUnbilledRevenueQry Engagement Project Unbilled Revenue Query

I_ProjDmndExpenseDistribution retrieves data of a demand for expense that is related to the distribution of the planned cost or revenue over time.

I_EngagementProjFinancialPlan - refer for financial plan of engagement projects. It would be beneficial to extract planned data relevant to cost and revenue.

I_EngmntProjActlPlanCube CDS view equips users with the necessary data to address several critical business questions, including identifying deviations between planned and actual costs to date, determining the proportion of billable versus non-billable project costs, and comparing current costs to baseline versions.

Hope this helps 

Thanks

Kind Regards

entry.title: Completed learning journey to get voucher under Digital Skills Initiative
entry.content:
Hi David @David_Chaviano 

Thank you David to guide Newcomers through the Digital Skills initiative.

I worked in IT Industry for 8 years and then had to have break because of various reasons. Now I want to pursue my career in IT again and thus did the self study for learning journey: Discovering SAP Business Technology Platform. I am a woman, originally from India and now moved to CANADA 2 years back. 

I would like to request you the voucher for FREE SAP Certification. Please find attached my learning journey completion certificate.

Regards,

Tripti

entry.title: Correct way to provision the destination for calling LLMs from SAP CAP applications GenAI Hub?
entry.content:
What is the correct way to provision a BTP destination for calling LLMs from SAP CAP applications on BTP to GenAI Hub viaSAP AI Core?

I'm using SAP CAP plugin

 

npm install cap-llm-plugin

 

Updating package.json in my CAP project with following:

 

"cds": {
    "requires": {
      "destinations": true,
      "cap-llm-plugin": true,
      "gen-ai-hub": {
        "anthropic--claude-3-sonnet": {
          "destinationName": "GenAIHubDestination",
          "deploymentUrl": "/v2/inference/deployments/d3b...6575",
          "resourceGroup": "default",
          "anthropic_version": "bedrock-2023-05-31",
          "max_tokens": 4096,
          "modelName": "anthropic--claude-3-sonnet"
        }
      },
      "GenAIHubDestination": {
        "kind": "rest",
        "credentials": {
          "destination": "ai_core_dest_4hybridmode",
          "requestTimeout": "300000"
        }
      }
    }
  }

 

Configured a Lite destination instance and  configured a new destination titled `ai_core_dest_4hybridmode` . Following tutorial here and used credentials from my AI core instance.

https://github.com/SAP-samples/codejam-cap-llm/tree/main/exercises/05-explore-destination-service#understand-how-a-destination-for-sap-ai-core-is-being-configured

I still get the following errors:

[odata] - GET /odata/v4/invokebedrock/invoke() 
[cds] - connect to cap-llm-plugin > cap-llm-plugin/srv/cap-llm-plugin.js 
[cds] - connect to GenAIHubDestination > rest { destination: 'ai_core_dest_4hybridmode', requestTimeout: '300000' }
[2024-11-14T20:23:24.425Z] WARN     (environment-accessor): Could not find service binding of type 'destination'. This might cause errors in other parts of the application.
[remote] - Error: Error during request to remote service: Failed to load destination.
    at run (/workspaces/capb2b/cap-llm-invoke/node_modules/@sap/cds/libx/_runtime/remote/utils/client.js:238:31)
    at process.processTicksAndRejections (node:internal/process/task_queues:95:5)
    at async RemoteService.on_handler (/workspaces/capb2b/cap-llm-invoke/node_modules/@sap/cds/libx/_runtime/remote/Service.js:274:20)
    at async next (/workspaces/capb2b/cap-llm-invoke/node_modules/@sap/cds/lib/srv/srv-dispatch.js:69:17)
    at async RemoteService.handle (/workspaces/capb2b/cap-llm-invoke/node_modules/@sap/cds/lib/srv/srv-dispatch.js:67:10)
    at async RemoteService.handle (/workspaces/capb2b/cap-llm-invoke/node_modules/@sap/cds/libx/_runtime/remote/Service.js:286:16)
    at async CAPLLMPlugin.getChatCompletionWithConfig (/workspaces/capb2b/cap-llm-invoke/node_modules/cap-llm-plugin/srv/cap-llm-plugin.js:255:24)
    at async ApplicationService.<anonymous> (/workspaces/capb2b/cap-llm-invoke/srv/invoke-service.js:35:26)
    at async next (/workspaces/capb2b/cap-llm-invoke/node_modules/@sap/cds/lib/srv/srv-dispatch.js:69:17)
    at async ApplicationService.handle (/workspaces/capb2b/cap-llm-invoke/node_modules/@sap/cds/lib/srv/srv-dispatch.js:67:10) {
  statusCode: 502,
  reason: {
    message: "Error during request to remote service: Failed to load destination. Caused by: Could not find service binding of type 'destination'.",
    request: {
      method: 'POST',
      url: '/v2/inference/deployments/d06914e9e0f063dd/invoke',
      headers: [Object]
    },
    correlationId: '29d9bdbe-2b92-42fc-9d17-d32b5776c1b0'
  }
}
[cds] - Error: Error during request to remote service: Failed to load destination.
    at run (/workspaces/capb2b/cap-llm-invoke/node_modules/@sap/cds/libx/_runtime/remote/utils/client.js:238:31)
    at process.processTicksAndRejections (node:internal/process/task_queues:95:5)
    at async RemoteService.on_handler (/workspaces/capb2b/cap-llm-invoke/node_modules/@sap/cds/libx/_runtime/remote/Service.js:274:20)
    at async next (/workspaces/capb2b/cap-llm-invoke/node_modules/@sap/cds/lib/srv/srv-dispatch.js:69:17)
    at async RemoteService.handle (/workspaces/capb2b/cap-llm-invoke/node_modules/@sap/cds/lib/srv/srv-dispatch.js:67:10)
    at async RemoteService.handle (/workspaces/capb2b/cap-llm-invoke/node_modules/@sap/cds/libx/_runtime/remote/Service.js:286:16)
    at async CAPLLMPlugin.getChatCompletionWithConfig (/workspaces/capb2b/cap-llm-invoke/node_modules/cap-llm-plugin/srv/cap-llm-plugin.js:255:24)
    at async ApplicationService.<anonymous> (/workspaces/capb2b/cap-llm-invoke/srv/invoke-service.js:35:26)
    at async next (/workspaces/capb2b/cap-llm-invoke/node_modules/@sap/cds/lib/srv/srv-dispatch.js:69:17)
    at async ApplicationService.handle (/workspaces/capb2b/cap-llm-invoke/node_modules/@sap/cds/lib/srv/srv-dispatch.js:67:10) {
  statusCode: 502,
  reason: {
    message: "Error during request to remote service: Failed to load destination. Caused by: Could not find service binding of type 'destination'.",
    request: {
      method: 'POST',
      url: '/v2/inference/deployments/d06914e9e0f063dd/invoke',
      headers: [Object]
    },
    correlationId: '29d9bdbe-2b92-42fc-9d17-d32b5776c1b0'
  }
}
[error] - 502 > {
  code: '502',
  message: 'Error during request to remote service: Failed to load destination.'
}

Any pointers?

entry.title: How to display 3 years data in Story based on User date Prompt in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Hi All,

Based on User selection, i have to lookback and display 3 years data in Story. I'm able to do it by using my date InputControl, but here the Current Time Interval is static, my requirement is to take the value from user selection and then look back to 3 years.

 

 

Could someone give me a clue on, how to do this? Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Search help restrictions fields are disabled for input.
entry.content:
Hi All,

When creating a very simple search my restrictions fields are disable for input.

This is the search (SE11):

And this is how the restriction columns appear when calling the help as a matchcode (or when testing it in SE11)

Any ideas?

TIA,

Luis

entry.title: Re: Microsoft Outlook and Teams Integration for Interview Scheduling
entry.content:
HI @Anant034 

I was wondering to ask you if we would like to enable the integration to do the testing process in preview environment is necesary to request to microsoft different credentials from production?

Thank you,

entry.title: GUI F4 help amodal
entry.content:
Hello SAP,

when calling the F4 help in control view (amodal), we cannot mark lines and rows in the resulting list (Ctrl Y, Ctrl C).

Netweaver SAP_BASIS    750    DIGITS
SAP GUI 8.00 64-bit patch level 8

 

Thank you for your help.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: GUI F4 help amodal
entry.content:
Hello customer,

Thank for you for reaching out to SAP Community.

The reason for the behavior you described is outlined in the KBA 1898861 - "Search Help displays incorrect layout".

Dialog (modal) will return an ABAP list and Control (amodal) will return an ALV. Thus, the two display will provide different interfaces.

entry.title: Re: Customized fields at Case management of FSCM
entry.content:
@Jigang_Zhang张吉刚 

Yes, that's what I did. I have created one custom field for the credit analyst, but this "Processor" filed is required to process the DCDs. Hence to keep it simple I wanted to just change the label back to its original/standard as "Processor", instead of hiding it.

This was changed by someone long back at field label to "Credit Analyst" but can't figure out from where/how they changed the label. Hence asking you 🙂.

Regards

Shouvick

entry.title: Re: Export Configuration CBC
entry.content:
Hello Annika98. In addition you can use Build Process Automation

You will be able to export the SSCUI configuration from your system using the Automation BOT and upload the SSCUI configuration to other system for approved list of SSCUI for the configuration marked as "File upload functionality enabled" = "Yes"

Please refer below on how to get started with SAP Build Process Automation Starter Pack

See below example for the automation bot in execution

entry.title: Re: Crystal Report Change Text interpretation runtime is...
entry.content: What versionof CR designer are you using? I testing in CR 2020 and it's not working...

entry.title: Profit center determination in service orders
entry.content:
Hi experts.

We are configuring the process "Corrective Service with Advanced Execution" (6AU). Every time we add a product in a service order the error "No profit center can be derived" appears, no matter what service product is added:

The substitution rule for CRM is already set:

The material has a profit center in the master data and the service organization also has a cost center assing:

 

But the profit center determination is failing. Has anyone had this error? What is the way to solve it. Thanks in advance.

Best regards.

David Avila.

entry.title: Re: SO automatically to PO in S/4 HANA cloud
entry.content:
Hi @RainaChu,

I would recommend reviewing the SAP Best Practices content as there are scenarios that can trigger a Purchase Order from a Sales Order. For example, Third-Party Order Processing and Advanced Intercompany Process.

In regard to the PO creation based on the PR, I would recommend using the Schedule Purchasing Jobs – Advanced app.

Hope this helps.

Arturo Montes de Oca

entry.title: Standard reports available with SAP SuccessFactors Workzone and ECSC?
entry.content: We will be launching shortly SAP SuccessFactors Workzone and ECSC. I am looking for a list of generic reports that I could use with these tools. Thank ou!

entry.title: Re: Restful ABAP - RAP Abstract Entity with readonly Para...
entry.content: Hi, Can make the fields on the abstract entity read only dynamically ( based on a condition )?

entry.title: B1if 2.0 step name missing in transaction monitor screen
entry.content:
Hi,

We encounter a strange problem in transaction monitor screen in SAP Business One integration framework 2.0. Normally I can see the step names in monitor screen. But now it only shows 'io' in the step field. Any idea how to get the 'Scenario step' name back in monitor?

Many thanks

Calvin

SAP Business One

entry.title: Re: SAP Ariba Validate And Enrich with SAP BTP Integration Suite – Part 1
entry.content:
Hi Krishna,

Thanks for the fantastic post on this topic!

I have a question regarding the Process Requisition Externally web service. Could this service be used to enrich a purchase requisition in SAP Ariba with additional data, such as supplier address or material details, directly from our S/4HANA backend system, without involving SAP BTP?

For example, if an Ariba requisition requires the supplier’s address from S/4HANA, can the service call S/4HANA directly to retrieve this data?

Alternatively, if BTP is required, could you provide guidance on how to fetch data from S/4HANA in this scenario to enrich the purchase requisition?

Any links or pointers would be greatly appreciated !

entry.title: Product type in S4 Cloud and Subscription Billing system
entry.content:
Hi, I am working on S4 cloud with Subscription billing scope item 57Z. The document says that you would need to use a product type of SBPD. And the item category group to be SBPD and SBRE. But I have seen that with the product type SERV and item category group SBPD you are still able to create Subscription contracts in Subscription Billing. Can someone let me know if there is a impact in the downstream. What is the standard approach defined by SAP.

In some cases we use product type SERV to enter Service contracts in S4 cloud.

thanks

Kiran

entry.title: Re: Updating Outbound Delivery Order in EWM and Outbound...
entry.content:
SAP does offer BAdI to do this. You can refer to this old SAP note. 

1281404 - Customer message log determination

I know this note works for EWM 9.4 but not sure for S4HANA but from the description, there is no conflicting and I believe it will work.

entry.title: Re: CDS View for Planned cost and revenue.
entry.content:
Hello SAP member,
 
The CDS view I_ENGMNTPROJTMEEXPNBILLGCUBE provides a way to access the Time and Expense Cap in the Engagement Project. This contains both Planned Cost and Actual Cost. Please check.
 
Technical Fields: PLNDCOSTAMT &
                            ACTLCOSTAMT
 
Thanks.
Regards,
Sravankumar Nendrambaka

entry.title: Re: From Tradition to Innovation: Upgrading to MRP Live
entry.content: Great summary of transition Classic to MRP Live for organisations sticking with core ERP/without strong sponsor or business case to implement IBP. Good to see SAP investing in standard functionality.

entry.title: Re: SAC Tunnel Live Connection SAP HANA error
entry.content: Hello Thanh Hieu,

entry.title: Re: CAP LLM Plugin – Empowering Developers for rapid Gen AI-CAP App Development
entry.content:
Hello,

Currently the plugin is available in nodejs. No current plan for support in java but will keep you posted if anything changes.

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: SAC Tunnel Live Connection SAP HANA error
entry.content: Hello Thanh Hieu, I have this issue, ¿did you manage to solve this problem?

entry.title: In a sales order, is it not possible, to delete more than one line at once?
